title: Slow Simmered Vietnamese Beef Pho
description: A fresh and gooey Vietnamese dish featuring unsalted beef stock and glaze, perfect for a lazy Sunday, and dairy-free-friendly. This recipe serves 4 people and can be prepared in under 3 hours.

ingredients.name: beef bones
ingredients.notes: preferably oxtail or beef neck bones

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roast the beef bones in the oven for 30 minutes.
In a large pot, combine the roasted beef bones, sliced onion, sliced ginger, star anise, and cinnamon sticks.
Pour in 4 quarts of water and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 1 hour.
Strain the stock through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot.
Add the fish sauce and soy sauce to the stock and simmer for another 10 minutes.
Cook the rice noodles according to the package instructions.
Slice the rare steak into thin strips.
Assemble the pho by placing some noodles into a bowl, adding a few slices of beef, and ladling the hot stock over the top.
